skill.name: data-exploration
skill.description: Guides DBX Studio users through schema discovery, table profiling, relationship review, and basic data quality checks.
skill.summary: Explore database schemas and profile tables with structured DBX Studio workflows.
skill.author: Dbxstudio

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged markdown backticks, SQL snippets, arrows, and percentage ranges as command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance indicators. Manual review found only DBX Studio tool names and read-only SQL profiling examples in SKILL.md, with no executable code, prompt injection, network access, or data exfiltration instructions.

content.value_statement: Teams need a repeatable way to understand unfamiliar databases before analysis or migration. This skill provides a structured DBX Studio workflow for schema discovery, profiling, relationship review, and data quality summaries.
actual_capabilities: Guides users through schema discovery with read_schema and describe_table.
Recommends table profiling steps for row counts, column types, sample data, and null rates.
Identifies likely relationships from foreign key naming patterns and common joins.
Provides SQL examples for row counts, null rates, distinct values, and date ranges.
Defines a structured summary format for tables, relationships, quality flags, and next queries.
limitations: Does not include executable scripts or automated database connectors.
Requires DBX Studio tools and database permissions supplied by the user environment.
Relationship discovery is heuristic and may miss constraints with unusual naming.
Sample data review may require privacy controls for sensitive datasets.

use_cases.title: Profile a New Dataset
use_cases.description: Create a first-pass inventory of tables, row counts, columns, sample records, and completeness signals.

use_cases.title: Map Table Relationships
use_cases.description: Review key columns and likely joins before building reports, dashboards, or transformations.

use_cases.title: Assess Data Readiness
use_cases.description: Summarize null rates, value distributions, and date coverage before planning cleanup work.

prompt_templates.title: Start Schema Discovery
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a database overview.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the data-exploration skill to inspect the public schema. List the tables, describe the most important tables, and summarize what each table appears to contain.

prompt_templates.title: Profile One Table
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the data-exploration workflow to profile the customer table. Include row count, column types, sample data observations, null rates, distinct value checks, and useful next questions.

prompt_templates.title: Find Relationships
prompt_templates.prompt: Explore the available tables and identify likely relationships. Look for id columns, common join patterns, and tables that appear to form business entities.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare an Exploration Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a structured exploration of the database and prepare a concise brief with table inventory, key relationships, data quality flags, date coverage, and recommended follow-up queries.

output_examples.input: Explore the public schema and summarize the main tables.
output: Table inventory with row counts and short table descriptions.
Key relationships inferred from shared identifier columns.
Data quality flags for sparse columns and missing date coverage.
Suggested follow-up questions for deeper analysis.

output_examples.input: Profile the orders table for reporting readiness.
output: Row count and main column groups.
Completeness summary using green, yellow, orange, and red scoring.
Top value distribution notes for status and category fields.
Date range and gaps that may affect reports.

output_examples.input: Help me understand how users, accounts, and events connect.
output: Relationship map described in plain language.
Likely join keys and tables that require verification.
Warnings for ambiguous identifiers or missing foreign key evidence.

best_practices: Start with schema discovery before requesting sample rows or detailed profiling.
Review sensitive columns before displaying sample data in shared conversations.
Verify inferred relationships against database constraints or domain knowledge.
anti_patterns: Do not treat inferred id column matches as confirmed relationships without validation.
Do not request broad sample data from sensitive production tables without a privacy review.
Do not skip null rate and date coverage checks when assessing reporting readiness.

faq.question: Does this skill run database queries automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides a workflow and example queries for DBX Studio-assisted exploration.

faq.question: Which tools does it reference?
faq.answer: It references DBX Studio actions such as read_schema, describe_table, get_table_stats, and get_table_data.

faq.question: Can it find table relationships?
faq.answer: It can suggest likely relationships from naming patterns, but users should verify them against constraints or business knowledge.

faq.question: Is it suitable for sensitive data?
faq.answer: It can be used with sensitive databases only when the user applies proper access controls and limits sample data exposure.

faq.question: Does it modify data?
faq.answer: No evidence found of write, update, delete, or administrative database operations in the skill instructions.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
